title: Authenticated Encryption with AES-GCM · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed security review reconstructs authenticated encryption with aes-gcm from threat, algorithm, parameter, implementation, key-custody, failure, test-vector, operations, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for versioned AES-GCM record envelope after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use AEAD Seal and Open as atomic confidentiality-and-integrity operations and release plaintext only after successful authentication.
Design a crash-, concurrency-, rollback-, and replication-safe GCM nonce strategy within the construction limits.
Bind protocol version, tenant, object identity, algorithm suite, direction, and other non-secret context as canonical associated data.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Crypto Go Aead Contract · changed case · Review Crypto Go Aead Contract
steps.instruction:
review case — A review cryptographic engineering team handles case 4877 during a signed release review. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a versioned AES-GCM envelope, nonce budget, AAD, lengths, and atomic plaintext release; preserve accessible status, non-color meaning, keyboard operation, error recovery, and secret-safe diagnostics; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use AEAD Seal and Open as atomic confidentiality-and-integrity operations and release plaintext only after successful authentication.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Collapse attacker-visible authentication failures while retaining safe internal classification and metrics.

hints: For Cryptography and Go Record what required effort · Review Crypto Go Gcm Failure Uniformity, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Crypto Go Gcm Failure Uniformity precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Cryptography and Go Record what required effort · Review Crypto Go Gcm Failure Uniformity, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review cryptographic engineering team handles case 3972 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a versioned AES-GCM envelope, nonce budget, AAD, lengths, and atomic plaintext release; cap bytes, work, memory, time, attempts, concurrency, and retained secret material before use; then repeat or roll back a nonce, sequence, or key version and reject the stale state. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Collapse attacker-visible authentication failures while retaining safe internal classification and metrics.

For Cryptography and Go Record what required effort · Review Crypto Go Gcm Failure Uniformity, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Detailed tag, key, nonce, and padding errors help clients recover securely.
